channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Matt Gaetz is dishing out career advice on Cameo
item.description: After announcing he would not return to Congress, Matt Gaetz is trying out a new career path: making personalized videos on Cameo.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok will block beauty filters for European teens following pushback
item.description: TikTok is planning to ban teen users in Europe from using beauty filters that alter their facial features.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Australian Senate debates social media ban for under-16s
item.description: The major parties' support for the ban all but guarantees the legislation will become law. But many child welfare and mental health advocates are concerned about unintended consequences.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Black Friday coupon scams are on the rise: here's how to avoid dodgy discount codes this sale season
item.description: As we head into Black Friday season, be mindful of coupon scams that could catch you off guard when hunting for bargains.
item.dc:creator: zac.kelly@futurenet.com (Zachariah Kelly), TechRadar

item.title: Coast Guard Enforces Cyber Requirements on Chinese-Made Ship-to-Shore Cranes
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard has implemented new cyber risk management requirements for Chinese-made ship-to-shore cranes, highlighting growing concerns over security risks posed by these cranes.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Data Privacy in the Age of AI: A New Model for Consent
item.description: Exploring the complexities of data privacy and consent in the age of artificial intelligence, with a focus on implementing point-in-time controls like swiping to enhance user agency.
item.dc:creator: Ron De Jesus, Fast Company

item.title: Here’s your last-minute guide to holiday social media etiquette
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Fox host: Podcasters like Rogan have 'earned' seats in White House briefing room
item.description: Fox News host Will Cain said popular podcasters like Joe Rogan have “earned” seats in the White House briefing room. When discussing the potential for a shake-up in the James S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Accelerating AI at scale without sacrificing security
item.description: How does a business stand out in a competitive market with AI? For some, it might be implementing a custom chatbot, or personalized recommendations built on advanced analytics and pushed out through a mobile app to customers.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Northern lights may be faintly visible across parts of the US this Thanksgiving
item.description: Solar storms may produce faint auroras across the northern rim of the United States this Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The key to operational AI: Modern data architecture
item.description: From customer service chatbots to marketing teams analyzing call center data, the majority of enterprises—about 90% according to recent data—have begun exploring AI. However, there’s a significant difference between those experimenting with AI and those fully integrating it into their operations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Fossilized dinosaur feces and vomit help scientists reconstruct the creatures' rise
item.description: Scientists are using used fossilized feces and vomit from Poland to reconstruct how dinosaurs came to dominate the Earth.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 6 sneaky gift card scams to watch out for this holiday season
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explores six sneaky holiday season gift card tricks, providing tips on how to outsmart scammers trying to rip you off.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: La Junta de Andalucía potencia el desarrollo de proyectos de digitalización mediante metodologías ágiles
item.description: La Sociedad Andaluza para el Desarrollo de las Telecomunicaciones (Sandetel) se apoya en la filial tecnológica del grupo Indra, Minsait, con el propósito de potenciar el desarrollo de proyectos de digitalización mediante metodologías ágiles y eficientes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Python to C: What’s new in Cython 3.1
item.description: Cython is the most convenient way to bridge C and Python languages and tools in Python programs. Developers use it to write C extensions or wrap C libraries for use in Python. Now, a major new release is on the way.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Transforming Brico Depot: A Journey to Digital Excellence
item.description: Brico Depot's ambitious journey towards digitalization, enhancing customer experience, and driving business success through strategic planning, cultural integration, and innovative technology adoption.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Australia’s lower House passes social media ban for under-16s
item.description: Communications Minister Michelle Rowland said the Senate would debate the bill later Wednesday. The major parties' support all but guarantees the legislation will pass in the Senate, where no party holds a majority of seats.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: 10 ways AI can make farmers’ jobs easier
item.description: For those who work the earth, AI can be an invaluable tool for making that work more efficient, sustainable and productive. AI helps farmers use the same tools they have been using for centuries, but with improved efficiency and focus.
item.dc:creator: Richard Baguley, Fast Company

item.title: Surveillance tech advances by Biden could aid in Trump's promised crackdown on immigration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will return to power next year with a raft of technological tools at his disposal that would help deliver his campaign promise of cracking down on immigration — among them, surveillance and artificial intelligence technology that the Biden administration already uses to
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Garance Burke And Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trends Shaping Digital Products in 2025 and Beyond
item.description: Consumer preferences, technology innovations, and emerging market forces shape digital products and their success in the market. Four trends cater to consumers in 2025 and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Brad Weber, Fast Company

item.title: This tiny design tweak made YouTube a more palatable place for women creators
item.description: It doesn’t take a genius to know that it’s hard to be a woman on the internet. From the sexism that mirrors women’s offline experiences to the objectification that’s part and parcel of being online, the environment for women is challenging at best.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Cerebras claims record in molecular dynamics simulations, says it’s 748x faster than Frontier supercomputer
item.description: Cerebras Systems, the maker of an AI “chip” the size of a pizza box, is making some impressive claims about its AI processing performance. At the recent Supercomputing 24 show, Cerebras announced a breakthrough in molecular dynamics simulations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Advanced programming with Java generics
item.description: Generics in Java enhance the type safety of your code and make it easier to read. In my last article, I introduced the general concepts of generics and showed examples from the Java Collections Framework. You also learned how to use generics to avoid runtime errors like the ClassCastException.
item.dc:creator: Info World
